meaning: adj.不能自制的,无节制的,荒淫的,[医]失禁的adv.仓促地,立即
examples: Many of our patients are incontinent.
His diseased bladder left him incontinent.
At first she was doubly incontinent, not now.
He was in any event severely crippled, even for a time paralysed and incontinent.
Conservative MP Richard Bacon, a member of the Commons Public Accounts Committee, said the MoD was "financially incontinent".
